title: 10 best herbal creams for jock itch

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tea tree creams helps with jock itch because of its potent antifungal properties. The cream's active ingredient, melaleuca oil, is derived from the leaves of the tea tree and has been shown to effectively combat fungal infections such as candida and malassezia. These fungi are often responsible for causing jock itch, and the cream works by killing them on contact, reducing symptoms like itching, redness, and inflammation, and promoting healthy skin.
remedy_properties: Tea Tree Oil (Melaleuca alternifolia): Has potent antifungal and antibacterial properties that help combat fungal infections such as jock itch by directly targeting the causative organisms.

Aloe Vera: Contains anti-inflammatory compounds like aloin and aloe-emodin, which soothe itching, redness, and irritation caused by jock itch, while also promoting wound healing and skin regeneration.

Calendula (Calendula officinalis): Exhibits antifungal and antibacterial properties that help reduce the severity of jock itch symptoms, including inflammation, itching, and crusting, while also promoting tissue repair and collagen production.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, which help to reduce inflammation and prevent fungal growth.
Leaves: The leaves contain high amounts of terpenoids, specifically cineole, which has antifungal and antibacterial properties that combat the fungi and bacteria that cause jock itch.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50 grams of fresh melaleuca alternifolia leaves and dry them completely in a warm oven for 2 hours.
Grind the dried leaves into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 5 minutes.
Combine 20 grams of the powdered melaleuca alternifolia with 50 grams of coconut oil and 10 grams of beeswax in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture over low heat, stirring occasionally, for 30 minutes until the beeswax is fully melted and the mixture is smooth.
Pour the cooled cream into sterilized jars and store them in the refrigerator for up to 6 weeks before using.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aloe creams helps with jock itch because of its exceptional soothing and anti-inflammatory properties. The gel inside the aloe vera leaf contains antioxidants, vitamins, and minerals that work together to reduce redness, itching, and inflammation caused by fungal infections like jock itch. Aloe cream can be applied directly to the affected area to calm the skin, promote healing, and prevent further irritation, providing relief from the uncomfortable symptoms associated with jock itch.
remedy_properties: Soothing and anti-inflammatory: Aloe vera's soothing properties help reduce inflammation and discomfort caused by jock itch, making it easier to manage symptoms.

Antifungal and antibacterial: Herbal aloe creams contain antifungal and antibacterial compounds that help combat fungal infections (like Candida) and bacterial overgrowth associated with jock itch, promoting healing and preventing further infection.

Moisturizing and barrier-forming: Aloe vera's moisturizing properties help to lock in moisture and create a protective barrier on the skin, reducing dryness and irritation that can exacerbate jock itch symptoms.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Aloe vera leaves contain anti-inflammatory and antifungal compounds, making them effective in soothing and treating fungal infections like jock itch.
Barks: The barks of the aloe plant contain bioactive compounds that help reduce inflammation, itching, and redness associated with jock itch, promoting healthy skin.
remedy_recipe: Gather fresh aloe vera leaves and extract their gel using a blender or juicer within 3 days of harvesting.
Combine the extracted aloe vera gel with 10% tea tree oil by weight and 5% neem oil by weight in a heat-proof bowl.
Heat the mixture gently over low heat for 20-30 minutes while stirring occasionally to facilitate the infusion process.
Remove the bowl from heat and let it cool down to room temperature before whipping the mixture with an electric mixer until smooth and creamy within 1 hour.
Transfer the cream into sterilized glass containers and store them in a cool dark place for up to 6 months or refrigerate for up to 2 weeks.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Purple coneflower contains compounds like caryophyllene and terpinen-4-ol, which have anti-inflammatory effects that can help reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with jock itch.

Antifungal properties: The cream's antifungal compounds, such as triterpenoids and flavonoids, help combat the fungal infection Candida albicans, which is a common cause of jock itch, by inhibiting its growth and reproduction.

Antioxidant properties: Purple coneflower's antioxidants, including polyphenols and flavonoids, help protect skin from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to the development and progression of jock itch.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in triterpenoid saponins, the roots help reduce fungal and bacterial infections that cause jock itch.
Barks: Containing phenolic compounds, the barks exhibit antifungal and anti-inflammatory properties that soothe itching and irritation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality dried echinacea purpurea flowers and measure out one ounce for every two cups of carrier oil.
Combine the measured echinacea flowers with the carrier oil in a clean glass jar and store it in a cool dark place for four weeks.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another container to remove the herbal particles and discard the solids.
Add one teaspoon of beeswax and five drops of tea tree essential oil to the infused oil mixture and heat it in a double boiler until the wax melts.
Remove from heat let cool slightly then whip the mixture with an electric mixer or whisk until it reaches your desired consistency for applying topically to affected areas.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Purple coneflower creams helps with jock itch because of its natural anti-fungal and antibacterial properties. The cream's active ingredient, Echinacea purpurea, has been shown to combat the fungus Trichophyton rubrum, which is responsible for most cases of jock itch. Additionally, purple coneflower's anti-inflammatory compounds help soothe itchy skin and reduce redness and irritation. As a result, using herbal purple coneflower creams can provide effective relief from the discomfort and embarrassment caused by jock itch, allowing users to feel more comfortable in their own skin.

Pot marigold creams helps with jock itch because they contain calendula, a natural antifungal agent that targets and eliminates the underlying fungus causing the infection. The cream's soothing properties also reduce itching, inflammation, and redness associated with jock itch, providing fast and effective relief. Additionally, calendula's antimicrobial properties prevent the growth of bacteria and other microorganisms that can exacerbate the condition, promoting a healthy and clear skin environment.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Pot marigold cream's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with jock itch, making it easier to manage symptoms.

Antimicrobial properties: The antimicrobial compounds in pot marigold cream work to combat fungal infections, such as Malassezia furfur, which causes jock itch, by inhibiting their growth and preventing further infection.
Emollient properties: Pot marigold cream's emollient nature helps soothe and moisturize the affected skin, reducing irritation and discomfort while also promoting healthy skin regeneration.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in soothing itchy skin and reducing redness.
Seeds: Containing essential fatty acids and vitamins, seeds help to moisturize and calm irritated skin, while also providing antioxidant benefits.
remedy_recipe: Harvest and dry 20-25 grams of fresh calendula flowers by air drying or oven drying at low heat for 2 hours.
Grind the dried calendula flowers into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle for 5 minutes.
Combine the ground calendula with 30 milliliters of carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil in a double boiler setup for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter to remove any impurities and achieve a smooth consistency within 1 hour.
Whip the calendula-infused carrier oil with 10 milliliters of beeswax and 2 milliliters of vitamin e oil for 5-7 minutes until a creamy texture forms.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sesame creams helps with jock itch because they contain antifungal and antibacterial properties that help to combat the underlying causes of the infection. The natural ingredients in herbal sesame creams, such as tea tree oil and aloe vera, work together to reduce inflammation, soothe itchy skin, and promote healthy healing. As a result, sesame creams can effectively alleviate symptoms of jock itch, including redness, itching, and rashes, providing quick relief for those affected by this common fungal infection.
remedy_properties: Antifungal properties: Sesame oil contains antifungal compounds like sesamin and sesamolin, which have been shown to inhibit the growth of fungal pathogens like Malassezia furfur, the fungus responsible for jock itch.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The antioxidants and polyphenols present in sesame oil have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with jock itch.

Wound-healing properties: Sesame oil's emollient and moisturizing properties can promote wound healing by creating a protective barrier on the skin, reducing friction and irritation that can exacerbate jock itch symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and antimicrobial compounds that help soothe and treat fungal infections.
Barks: Contain anti-inflammatory properties that reduce redness and swelling associated with jock itch.
remedy_recipe: Grind 50 grams of dried sesamum indicum seeds into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 5 minutes.
Boil 200 milliliters of distilled water with 10 grams of powdered camphor for 10 minutes to create an infusion.
Combine the ground sesamum indicum powder with the cooled camphor-infused water mixture, stirring until it forms a smooth paste.
Apply the herbal cream to affected areas twice daily, using a gentle massaging motion for 2-3 minutes each application.
Store the prepared herbal cream in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 7 days or refrigerate for longer use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile creams helps with jock itch because of its anti-inflammatory, antifungal, and soothing properties. The cream's ingredients, such as apigenin and bisabolol, work together to reduce redness, itching, and swelling associated with fungal infections like jock itch. Chamomile's natural calming effects also promote a healthy skin environment, reducing irritation and promoting healing. By applying the cream directly to affected areas, users can experience quick relief from symptoms and faster recovery times. This makes chamomile creams an effective and natural remedy for treating jock itch.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Chamomile's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as apigenin and luteolin, help reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with jock itch.

Antifungal properties: The flavonoids and terpenes in chamomile have been shown to inhibit the growth of fungal organisms that cause jock itch, such as Malassezia furfur and Trichophyton mentagrophytes.

Soothing and calming effects: Chamomile's flavonoids and essential oils help calm irritated skin, reduce discomfort, and promote a sense of relaxation and reduced stress – all of which can exacerbate jock itch symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective against fungal infections and soothing irritated skin.
Flowers: Containing apigenin, an antioxidant with antifungal and antibacterial properties, which helps combat jock itch-causing fungi and bacteria.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh matricaria chamomilla flowers at dawn or in the late afternoon to ensure optimal essential oil content.
Dry the harvested flowers by spreading them on paper towels or clean cloth for 24 hours, then store them in airtight containers.
Combine 20 grams of dried matricaria chamomilla flowers with 100 milliliters of organic sweet almond oil and heat gently for 30 minutes at low temperature.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth into a clean glass container, squeezing the herbs to release all the infused oil, then allow it to cool completely.
Whip the cooled infused oil with 20 grams of beeswax and 10 milliliters of coconut oil until smooth, creamy consistency is achieved within 15 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wintergreen creams helps with jock itch because of their antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The menthol content in wintergreen creams creates a cooling sensation on the skin, reducing itching and discomfort caused by fungal infections such as candida and malassezia. Additionally, the camphor present in these creams helps to reduce redness and swelling associated with jock itch, promoting a faster recovery and relieving symptoms. As a result, wintergreen creams provide effective relief from the annoying symptoms of jock itch, allowing individuals to regain their comfort and confidence.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Wintergreen cream's salicylic acid content helps reduce inflammation and itching associated with jock itch, providing relief to affected skin areas.
Antifungal properties: The menthol in wintergreen cream disrupts fungal growth, inhibiting the spread of Candida albicans and other fungi that cause jock itch.
Antibacterial properties: Wintergreen's eucalyptol content has antibacterial properties, which help combat bacterial infections that can exacerbate or complicate jock itch.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the wintergreen plant have been traditionally used to make antifungal and antibacterial creams, helping to combat fungal infections such as jock itch.
Barks: Wintergreen barks contain salicylic acid, a natural anti-inflammatory compound that helps soothe and reduce itching associated with jock itch.
remedy_recipe: Harvest gaultheria procumbens leaves and stems, ensuring 2 tablespoons of dried material.
Steep the dried plant material in 1 cup of boiling water for 10-15 minutes, yielding 20 milliliters of infusion.
Combine the infused water with 50 grams of petroleum jelly and 30 grams of beeswax, melting the mixture at 100 degrees celsius.
Add 5% tea tree oil and 2% lavender oil to the melted mixture, stirring well for 1 minute.
Pour the cream into sterilized containers, allowing it to cool and solidify within 30-40 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: American witch hazel creams helps with jock itch because of its natural anti-inflammatory and antifungal properties. The cream's active ingredients, such as salicylic acid and witch hazel extract, work together to reduce redness, itching, and inflammation caused by fungal infections like athlete's foot and ringworm. Additionally, the astringent properties help to dry out the affected area, preventing the growth of new fungal cells and promoting healthy skin.
remedy_properties: Astringent Properties: Witch Hazel's astringent properties help to dry out and reduce itching and inflammation caused by Jock Itch, making it an effective antifungal agent.

Antimicrobial Properties: The antimicrobial compounds in Witch Hazel cream, such as tannins and polyphenols, inhibit the growth of fungi and bacteria that cause Jock Itch, preventing further infection and promoting healing.
Anti-inflammatory Properties: Witch Hazel's anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce redness, swelling, and irritation associated with Jock Itch, providing relief from discomfort and pain.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in tannins, which provide antifungal and antibacterial properties to help soothe and treat fungal infections like jock itch.
Barks: Contain salicylic acid, a natural anti-inflammatory that helps reduce itching and irritation associated with jock itch.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh witch hazel leaves and flowers for infusion.
Filter the infused liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter.
Combine the herbal extract with other ingredients and oils.
Melt the mixture using a double boiler or in the microwave.
Fill sterilized jars and let the cream cool and set.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Onion creams helps with jock itch because they possess natural antifungal and antibacterial properties that work to combat the underlying causes of the condition. The active ingredients in herbal onion creams, such as sulfur compounds and flavonoids, penetrate deep into the skin to target fungal infections like ringworm, while also reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with jock itch. This natural approach can help promote healing and alleviate symptoms without harsh chemicals or artificial fragrances.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial compounds: The antimicrobial properties of onion cream help to combat fungal infections such as candida and yeast, which are common causes of jock itch, by preventing their growth and reproduction.

Anti-inflammatory agents: Onion cream's anti-inflammatory properties reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with jock itch by decreasing the production of prostaglandins, which are chemicals that promote inflammation.
Antioxidants: The antioxidant properties of onion cream help to protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals, which can exacerbate jock itch symptoms such as irritation and discoloration.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots contain sulfur compounds, which have antibacterial and antifungal properties that help combat fungal infections like jock itch.
Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes, the underground stems of the onion plant, have anti-inflammatory properties that can soothe irritated skin and reduce redness associated with jock itch.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10 grams of fresh onion bulbs allium cepa and dry them for 7 days in a warm dark place.
Grind the dried onions into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle or coffee grinder for 5 minutes.
Mix 20 grams of coconut oil with 15 grams of beeswax and 5 grams of shea butter in a double boiler for 10 minutes.
Add 10 grams of ground onion powder to the melted mixture and stir well for 2 minutes.
Pour the mixture into sterilized glass containers and let it cool and set at room temperature for 30 minutes before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemongrass creams helps with jock itch because of its antifungal properties, which effectively combat the underlying fungal infections that cause the condition. The cream's anti-inflammatory and soothing qualities also help to reduce redness, itching, and discomfort associated with jock itch. Additionally, lemongrass' natural antibacterial properties prevent bacterial overgrowth and promote a healthy skin environment, allowing for faster healing and reduced risk of recurrence. As a result, lemongrass creams provide effective relief from the symptoms of jock itch, promoting overall comfort and well-being.
remedy_properties: Antifungal properties: Lemongrass contains compounds like citral and geraniol, which have antifungal activity against Candida albicans, the fungus that causes jock itch, helping to reduce inflammation and promote healing.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The flavonoids present in lemongrass, such as quercetin, possess anti-inflammatory effects, which help to soothe itching, redness, and swelling associated with jock itch, reducing discomfort and promoting a quicker recovery.

Antioxidant properties: Lemongrass is rich in antioxidants like vitamin C and beta-carotene, which help protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals, reducing oxidative stress and promoting a healthy environment for wound healing and tissue repair.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, lemongrass roots help soothe and calm itchy skin.
Rhizomes: Containing a high concentration of citral, a natural antifungal agent, rhizomes effectively combat fungal infections causing jock itch.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh cymbopogon citratus leaves from your garden or store them in an airtight container for future use.
Dry the harvested leaves by spreading them on a paper towel-lined baking sheet and letting them air dry for 24 hours.
Combine dried cymbopogon citratus leaves with coconut oil in a 1:2 ratio and allow it to infuse for 4-6 weeks, shaking daily.
Strain the infused mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass jar, discarding solids and storing the liquid in the refrigerator for up to 2 weeks.
Mix the herbal extract with beeswax and shea butter in a 1:1 ratio, warming the mixture gently over low heat until fully incorporated and smooth, taking approximately 15-20 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wormwood creams helps with jock itch because of its potent antifungal and anti-inflammatory properties. The cream's active ingredient, thujone, targets fungal infections such as Candida, which is a common cause of jock itch. Additionally, wormwood's antiseptic properties help to reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with the condition. As the cream penetrates deep into the skin, it provides long-lasting relief from discomfort and promotes a healthy environment for wound healing, making it an effective treatment option for individuals suffering from jock itch.
remedy_properties: Antifungal: Wormwood's antifungal properties help combat the fungal infection that causes jock itch, reducing inflammation and promoting healing.
Anti-inflammatory: Wormwood's anti-inflammatory compounds reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with jock itch, providing relief to affected areas.
Antiparasitic: Wormwood's antiparasitic properties help eliminate the fungal parasite responsible for jock itch, reducing the risk of recurrence and promoting overall skin health.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of wormwood are rich in bioactive compounds that help combat fungal infections and soothe itchy skin.
Barks: The barks of wormwood contain flavonoids and phenolic acids that provide antifungal and anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective against jock itch.
remedy_recipe: Gather high-quality dried artemisia absinthium leaves, aloe vera gel, coconut oil, beeswax, and vitamin e oil.
Combine one part dried artemisia absinthium leaves with three parts coconut oil in a clean glass jar and let it infuse for 14 days.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into another glass jar and discard the solids before adding two tablespoons of beeswax and heating until melted.
Remove from heat, add one teaspoon of aloe vera gel and vitamin e oil, and stir well to combine before pouring into sterilized jars.
Allow the cream to cool and thicken completely before use, which should take approximately 30 minutes to an hour.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Goldenseal creams helps with jock itch because of its potent antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties. The cream's active ingredient, berberine, targets and kills the fungus that causes jock itch, reducing the risk of infection and promoting healthy skin. Additionally, goldenseal's anti-inflammatory properties help to soothe itchy and irritated skin, providing quick relief from discomfort. By addressing both the fungal infection and inflammation simultaneously, goldenseal creams offer a comprehensive solution for effectively treating jock itch.
remedy_properties: Berberine: Goldenseal contains berberine, a natural antibacterial and antifungal compound that helps combat fungal infections like jock itch.
Hydrophobic lipids: The cream's hydrophobic (water-repelling) lipids help lock in moisture and reduce fungal growth, while also providing a soothing barrier against irritation.
Anti-inflammatory compounds: Goldenseal's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as berberine and isothiocyanates, may help reduce redness, itching, and swelling associated with jock itch.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in berberine, an antimicrobial compound that helps to reduce fungal and bacterial infections associated with jock itch.
Rhyzomes: Contain a higher concentration of alkaloids, which provide anti-inflammatory and antifungal properties to soothe itchy skin and combat infection.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2 ounces of dried goldenseal root powder and 4 tablespoons of coconut oil.
Combine the goldenseal powder with 8 tablespoons of sweet almond oil in a heatproof glass bowl.
Place the mixture in a double boiler or a shallow metal pan set over simmering water for 30 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass bottle and discard solids.
Allow the cream to cool and thicken at room temperature for several hours before using.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion creams helps with jock itch because of its natural anti-inflammatory and antifungal properties. The cream's active ingredients, such as taraxasterol and saponins, work together to soothe itchy skin and reduce redness and irritation caused by fungal infections like ringworm. Additionally, dandelion's antimicrobial properties help to combat the growth of fungus and bacteria that can contribute to jock itch. By reducing inflammation and combating infection, dandelion creams provide effective relief from itching and discomfort associated with this common condition.
remedy_properties: Antifungal properties: Dandelion's antifungal compounds help combat the fungal infection Candida albicans, which is responsible for jock itch.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in dandelion creams reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with jock itch, providing relief from discomfort.
Antioxidant properties: Dandelion's antioxidants help soothe and protect the affected skin, reducing damage caused by free radicals and promoting healthy tissue repair.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in soothing and calming irritated skin.
Rhyzomes: Containing high amounts of sesquiterpenes, which have been shown to exhibit antifungal and antibacterial properties, ideal for combating fungal infections like jock itch.
remedy_recipe: Harvest and clean taraxacum officinale leaves for infusion taking approximately one hour.
Combine 20 grams of dried taraxacum officinale leaves with 200 milliliters of boiling distilled water steeping for 30 minutes.
Strain the infused liquid discarding the solids and combining it with 100 grams of cold-pressed coconut oil mixing thoroughly.
Add 5 percent tea tree essential oil by volume to the infusion-oil mixture blending well taking approximately five minutes.
Pour the herbal cream into sterilized glass jars sealing tightly and labeling accurately storing in a cool dark place.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sweet briar creams helps with jock itch because of its antifungal and antibacterial properties. The cream's active ingredients, such as salicylic acid and tea tree oil, work together to combat fungal and bacterial infections that cause itching, redness, and inflammation in the groin area. By reducing the growth of these microorganisms, sweet briar creams help alleviate symptoms and promote healing, providing effective relief from jock itch discomfort. This natural solution offers a gentle yet powerful approach to addressing this common condition.
remedy_properties: Antifungal properties: Sweet briar cream contains antifungal compounds like salicylic acid and tannins that help combat fungal infections like jock itch, reducing inflammation and itching.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids in sweet briar cream helps to reduce swelling and redness associated with jock itch, providing relief from discomfort and pain.

Antimicrobial properties: Sweet briar's antimicrobial compounds, including terpenes and volatile oils, work together to inhibit the growth of bacteria and fungi that contribute to jock itch, promoting healing and preventing further infection.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in anti-inflammatory and antifungal compounds, making them effective against fungal infections causing jock itch.
Rhyzomes: Contain antibacterial properties that help combat bacterial infections contributing to jock itch, while also soothing skin irritation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest rosa rubiginosa petals from healthy plants and dry them thoroughly in a warm, dark place for 7-10 days.
Grind 50 grams of dried rosa rubiginosa petals into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle or coffee grinder.
Mix the powdered rosa rubiginosa with 100 milliliters of cooled coconut oil and 20 milliliters of beeswax in a double boiler, heating until fully incorporated.
Add 10 drops of tea tree essential oil for its antifungal properties and stir well to combine, allowing the mixture to cool and solidify at room temperature.
Transfer the cooled cream to glass jars with tight-fitting lids and store in a cool, dry place for up to six months or refrigerate for up to one year.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ceylon cinnamon creams helps with jock itch because of its unique combination of anti-inflammatory, antifungal, and antibacterial properties. The cream's active compounds, such as cinnamaldehyde, work together to reduce itching, redness, and swelling associated with fungal infections like jock itch. Additionally, Ceylon cinnamon's natural astringent properties help to dry out the affected area, preventing further moisture buildup that can exacerbate the condition. As a result, the cream provides fast and effective relief from the discomfort of jock itch, promoting healthy skin and a reduced risk of recurrence.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Herbal Ceylon cinnamon contains compounds like cinnamaldehyde, which has been shown to inhibit the growth of fungal and bacterial pathogens responsible for jock itch, reducing inflammation and promoting healing.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The active compounds in Ceylon cinnamon, such as cinnamic acid, have anti-inflammatory effects that can help soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing redness and itching associated with jock itch.

Antioxidant properties: Herbal Ceylon cinnamon contains a high amount of antioxidants, which can help protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals and oxidative stress, promoting overall skin health and wound healing.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes, also known as underground stems, contain high amounts of cinnamaldehyde, a natural antimicrobial agent that helps combat fungal infections causing jock itch.
Barks: The barks of the Ceylon cinnamon plant have been used in traditional medicine to treat skin conditions like jock itch, thanks to their antifungal and antibacterial properties.
remedy_recipe: Grind 20 grams of dried cinnamomum verum bark into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Combine the ground cinnamon with 10 grams of coconut oil in a double boiler set over medium heat.
Stir the mixture until it reaches a temperature of 60°c to 70°c, then remove from heat and let cool slightly.
Add 5 grams of beeswax to the mixture and stir until melted and fully incorporated.
Pour the mixture into small tin containers or jars, allowing it to cool and solidify completely at room temperature for about 30 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tasmanian blue gum creams helps with jock itch because of its natural anti-fungal and antibacterial properties. The cream's active ingredients, such as eucalyptol and menthol, create a soothing and cooling sensation on the skin, reducing itching and inflammation. Additionally, the antimicrobial compounds in Tasmanian blue gum work to inhibit the growth of fungi and bacteria that cause jock itch, promoting healthy skin and preventing recurrence of the condition. As a result, herbal Tasmanian blue gum creams provide effective relief for those suffering from jock itch.
remedy_properties: Antifungal property: Tasmanian Blue Gum cream has been shown to exhibit antifungal activity, which helps combat the fungal infection Candida albicans that causes jock itch.
Anti-inflammatory property: The cream's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with jock itch, providing relief from uncomfortable symptoms.
Antimicrobial property: Tasmanian Blue Gum's antimicrobial properties help prevent bacterial and fungal growth, promoting a healthy environment for the skin to recover from jock itch.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the Tasmanian Blue Gum contain compounds that exhibit antifungal and anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in treating fungal infections like jock itch.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes, which are underground stems that store nutrients, have been found to possess antibacterial and antiviral activities, helping to combat the underlying causes of jock itch.
remedy_recipe: Sterilize glass jars and utensils in boiling water for at least 20 minutes.
Combine 250ml of distilled water with 25g of dried eucalyptus globulus leaves in a heat-resistant mixing bowl.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler or a saucepan set over simmering water for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the infusion through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another container discarding solids and allowing to cool.
Mix 20g of beeswax pellets with the cooled infusion and whip until smooth and creamy taking approximately 5 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender creams helps with jock itch because of its antifungal and anti-inflammatory properties. The cream's active ingredients, including linalool and linalyl acetate, work together to combat the fungal infection causing jock itch, reducing redness, itching, and irritation. Additionally, the cream's soothing and calming effects help to alleviate discomfort and promote a sense of relaxation, making it an effective and natural remedy for treating this common skin condition.
remedy_properties: Antifungal properties: Lavender oil's antifungal properties help combat fungal infections such as candida and athlete's foot, which can contribute to jock itch.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Lavender oil's anti-inflammatory properties reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with jock itch, providing relief from discomfort and promoting healing.

Antimicrobial properties: Lavender oil's antimicrobial properties inhibit the growth of bacteria, fungi, and other microorganisms that can exacerbate jock itch, preventing further infection and speeding up recovery.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in soothing and calming irritated skin.
Flowers: Containing linalool and linalyl acetate, these essential oils help reduce fungal infections and promote a healthy skin environment.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20g of dried lavandula angustifolia flowers and grind them into a fine powder using a spice grinder for 2 minutes.
Combine the powdered lavandula angustifolia with 50g of coconut oil in a heat-proof glass bowl and stir well to mix.
Place the mixture in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water and melt the coconut oil completely stirring occasionally for 10 minutes.
Remove the melted coconut oil from the heat and let it cool to around 60°c before adding 5g of beeswax and stirring until fully incorporated taking about 3 minutes.
Pour the cooled mixture into a sterilized glass jar and seal tightly allowing it to solidify at room temperature for 30 minutes before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: White willow creams helps with jock itch because they contain salicin, a natural anti-inflammatory compound that reduces redness and swelling associated with the condition. The cream's antibacterial properties also help to combat fungal infections, which are common causes of jock itch. Additionally, the soothing and moisturizing effects of the cream can help to calm irritated skin, promoting healing and preventing further irritation. As a result, white willow creams provide effective relief from the discomfort and itching associated with jock itch.
remedy_properties: Salicin: This compound has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with jock itch.
Tannins: Tannins have antifungal and antibacterial properties, which can help combat the fungal or bacterial infections that cause jock itch and prevent their spread.
Methyl Salicylate: This compound has analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help relieve itching, burning, and discomfort caused by jock itch.
remedy_parts: Roots: Contains salicylic acid, which helps to reduce inflammation and itching associated with jock itch.
Barks: Rich in salicin, a compound that has antifungal and anti-inflammatory properties, making it effective against fungal infections like jock itch.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of dried white willow bark salix alba and grind into a fine powder using a spice grinder.
Combine the ground white willow bark powder with 20ml of organic coconut oil in a small saucepan over low heat stirring constantly for 30 minutes.
Add 5ml of pure beeswax to the mixture and stir until fully incorporated and melted within 10-15 minutes.
Remove from heat and let cool slightly before whipping the mixture into a smooth cream using an electric mixer for 2-3 minutes.
Pour the salix alba cream into sterilized glass jars filling to the top and seal immediately while still warm.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for jock itch are topical treatments that utilize natural ingredients to soothe and alleviate symptoms of tinea cruris, also known as jock itch. These creams harness the antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties of herbs like tea tree oil, chamomile, and aloe vera to reduce itching, redness, and irritation. Examples of effective herbal creams include those containing calendula, burdock root, and neem extract. By using these natural remedies, individuals can find relief from the discomfort and embarrassment associated with jock itch, allowing them to enjoy physical activities and daily life without restriction.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat jock itch with herbal creams is to apply a cream containing ingredients such as tea tree oil, aloe vera, and coconut oil directly to the affected area. These herbs have antifungal properties that help to combat the infection, reducing redness, itching, and inflammation. Apply the cream twice daily, ensuring it covers the entire affected area for optimal results.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to jock itch that are treated with herbal creams are ringworm, athlete's foot, and eczema. Herbal creams containing tea tree oil, neem oil, and aloe vera have been effective in treating fungal infections like these. These creams work by reducing inflammation, killing fungal spores, and promoting skin healing. Additionally, some herbal creams may also help soothe itchiness and redness associated with these conditions.
